column4: Procurement of Chemistry Laboratory Equipment (to meet the minimum requirements of CMO No.47, series of 2017)
column7:
During the recent visit conducted by the Office of Programs and Standards Development (OPSD), several critical recommendations were raised regarding the current condition and adequacy of the laboratory facilities of the College of Science (COS).

The evaluators noted that a number of essential laboratory equipment and instruments required for laboratory courses, as stipulated in CHED Memorandum Order No. 47, s. 2017 (CMO 47, s. 2017) for the Bachelor of Science in Chemistry—currently being used as a reference in evaluating the Bachelor of Applied Science in Laboratory Technology (BASLT) program—are not yet available in the laboratories. These include, but are not limited to, a refractometer, laboratory shaker, electrophoresis apparatus, melting point apparatus, polymerase chain reaction (PCR) apparatus, polarimeter, tensiometer, viscometer, vortex mixer, constant-volume and pressure, calorimeter, portable FTIR and SEM. Consequently, there is an urgent need to procure additional laboratory equipment and instruments to ensure compliance with the minimum requirements prescribed in the CMO.

Furthermore, based on the results of the evaluation conducted by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED), the application for the issuance of the Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC) cannot be favorably considered at present. The institution was likewise advised not to operate the program without the appropriate authority from the Commission. In view of this, the acquisition of the required laboratory equipment and instruments becomes imperative should the Technological University of the Philippines pursue the restructuring and eventual implementation of the BASLT-related program.

In response to these findings and recommendations, the College of Science respectfully proposes the procurement of the aforementioned laboratory equipment and the improvement of laboratory facilities. These initiatives aim to ensure compliance with regulatory standards, facilitate the issuance of the COPC, strengthen instructional delivery, and support the continuous improvement of laboratory-based academic programs.
column9:
To procure the required laboratory equipment and upgrade the laboratory facilities of the College of Science (COS) in order to comply with the standards prescribed in CHED Memorandum Order No. 47, s. 2017 and the recommendations of the Office of Programs and Standards Development. This initiative is essential to ensure institutional readiness should the Technological University of the Philippines pursue the restructuring and eventual implementation of the Bachelor of Applied Science in Laboratory Technology (BASLT)–related program (i.e., BS Chemistry)

Specific Objectives:

1. To procure essential laboratory equipment and instruments required for laboratory courses, including but not limited to a refractometer, laboratory shaker, electrophoresis apparatus, melting point apparatus, polymerase chain reaction (PCR) apparatus, polarimeter, tensiometer, viscometer, vortex mixer, constant pressure calorimeter, constant-volume calorimeter, portable Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), and scanning electron microscope (SEM). 

2. To strengthen the laboratory capabilities of the institution in support of the restructuring and future implementation of the BASLT- related program at the Technological University of the Philippines.

3. To enhance the quality of laboratory instruction, practical training, and research opportunities for students through the provision of adequate and updated laboratory equipment and facilities.

column4: Multi pupose (parking/offices/commercial spaces) building
column7:
The development shall be a multi-storey, mixed-use facility designed to optimize land utilization while addressing the growing infrastructural demands of the University. The building is envisioned to include:
Ground and Lower Floors:
Structured parking facility for students, faculty, staff, and visitors
Traffic management system and security provisions
Intermediate Floors:
Commercial spaces (e.g., food outlets, bookstores, service centers, and partner-operated facilities)
Spaces for industry linkage offices and innovation hubs
Upper Floors:
Multi-purpose halls and function rooms for academic, research, and university events
Flexible learning spaces and conference facilities
The facility shall adopt modern, sustainable, and resilient building standards, incorporating:
Energy-efficient systems
Disaster-resilient structural design
Smart building technologies
Accessibility-compliant features
The project may be implemented through government funding

The proposed Multi-Purpose Building is anchored on the University’s commitment to modernization, resource optimization, and financial sustainability, while supporting its mandate as a premier state university in technology and applied sciences.
column9:
1. Addressing Critical Infrastructure Gaps
TUP currently faces limited parking capacity, insufficient multi-purpose venues, and fragmented commercial services, which constrain campus operations and negatively affect stakeholder experience. This project directly addresses these gaps by consolidating essential functions into a single, efficient facility.

2. Efficient Land Utilization
Given the limited available land within TUP campuses, vertical development through a mixed-use structure maximizes space efficiency while preserving open areas for future academic expansion.

3. Enhancement of Student and Faculty Welfare
The inclusion of accessible commercial spaces and service areas will improve daily campus life by providing:
Affordable food options
Academic support services
Convenient access to essential goods

4. Strengthening Industry Linkages and Innovation Ecosystem
Dedicated spaces within the building may serve as innovation hubs, incubation centers, and industry collaboration zones, supporting TUP’s thrust toward research commercialization, entrepreneurship, and internationalization.

5. Revenue Generation and Financial Sustainability
The commercial component of the project presents an opportunity for internally generated income (IGI) through leasing and partnerships, which can:
Support university operations
Fund academic and research initiatives
Reduce reliance on government subsidy

6. Support to Institutional Events and Academic Functions
The multi-purpose halls will provide dedicated venues for university-wide activities, including:
Academic conferences
Cultural and student events
Extension and community engagement programs

7. Alignment with Strategic and National Development Goals
The project aligns with:
TUP’s vision of becoming a globally competitive technological university
CHED directives on infrastructure modernization and quality education delivery
National goals on sustainable and smart campus development

In essence, the project is not only a response to physical infrastructure needs but also a strategic investment in the institution’s future, ensuring that both learners and educators are provided with spaces that inspire growth, efficiency, and excellence.

Furthermore, improved classrooms foster a more engaging and interactive learning atmosphere, while enhanced office spaces contribute to increased staff productivity, collaboration, and morale.

Mezzanine Area eXpansion for Structured Planning and Area Capacity Enhancement
column7: a
column8: RQAT, Institutional Infrastructure Improvement and Operational Efficiency Enhancement, including Space Optimization and Document Storage Management (ETEEAP Office)Institutional Infrastructure Improvement and Operational Efficiency Enhancement, including Space Optimization and Document Storage Management (ETEEAP Office)
column9: The proposed M.A.X. S.P.A.C.E. (Mezzanine Area eXpansion for Strategic Planning and Area Capacity Enhancement) project is essential to address the growing space limitations within the ETEEAP Office, driven by the increasing number of applicants, assessment activities, and document processing requirements. The current office layout is no longer sufficient to support efficient workflow, resulting in congestion, limited workstations, and challenges in managing and securing voluminous records. This project will maximize vertical space through the development of a mezzanine area, enabling the creation of additional workspaces, dedicated assessment areas, and organized document storage systems. It will significantly improve operational efficiency, enhance records management, and provide a more conducive working and service environment for staff, assessors, and clients. While primarily an infrastructure and operational enhancement initiative, the project also contributes to the university’s compliance with Recognition of Quality Assurance in Teaching and Learning (RQAT) standards by strengthening support systems, improving administrative processes, and ensuring systematic records management. By optimizing space utilization, the university can ensure sustainable and efficient operations of the ETEEAP Office while reinforcing its commitment to quality service delivery and continuous improvement.
column10:
The implementation of the M.A.X. S.P.A.C.E. (Mezzanine Area eXpansion for Strategic Planning and Area Capacity Enhancement) project will provide significant benefits to the ETEEAP Office and the university as a whole. It will result in improved space utilization by maximizing vertical areas, leading to increased workspace capacity for staff and assessors. The project will enhance operational efficiency by reducing congestion and streamlining workflow processes.

Additionally, it will strengthen document storage and records management through organized, secure, and accessible filing systems, ensuring compliance with audit and quality assurance requirements. The creation of dedicated areas for assessment, meetings, and administrative functions will improve service delivery and client experience. Overall, the project will contribute to a more professional, safe, and conducive working environment while supporting institutional growth and continuous quality improvement.

column4: Installation of Hand railings at COS/CLA Building
column7: Installation of handrailing at COS/CLA Building
column9: PWD handrail infrastructure, designed to ensure accessibility for persons with disabilities (PWDs), requires strict adherence to specific standards regarding height, continuity, material, and installation, largely guided by laws like BP 344 in the Philippines. Handrails must be installed on both sides of ramps and stairs, featuring double-railing systems and specific extensions to provide support for both wheelchair users and ambulant disabled individuals
